caption: Finding
description: The Finding event is a generic event that defines a set of attributes available in the Findings category.
name: finding

activity_id.description: The normalized identifier of the finding activity. Use 1 (Create) when a finding is first generated, 2 (Update) when an existing finding is modified (e.g., severity change, new evidence), and 3 (Close) when a finding is resolved or dismissed.

activity_name.description: The finding activity name, as defined by the activity_id. When activity_id is 99 (Other), this must contain the source-specific activity label.

comment.description: A user provided comment about the finding.

device.description:
Describes the device/host relevant to the finding.

If applicable, it can be used in conjunction with resources. e.g. The role of the device as the target or the actor, its IP address etc.

If a single device is the source of the finding, rather than a targeted or affected device, use the Host profile and populate device.

devices.description:
Describes the devices/hosts relevant to the finding.

If applicable, they can be used in conjunction with resources. e.g. The roles of the devices as targets or actors, their IP addresses etc.

If a single device is the source of the finding, use the Host profile and populate device.

end_time.description: The time of the most recent event or finding that contributed to this finding.

endpoint_connections.description:
Describes the affected endpoint network connections, or network connection attempts.

If applicable, they can be used in conjunction with resources. e.g. The roles of the endpoints as targets or actors, their hostnames etc.

is_aggregate.description:
If set, indicates that the finding aggregates other findings. See finding_info.related_events when this value is true and the related events are findings.

Note that aggregated findings are similar to the Incident Finding if the Incident profile is applied.

notes.caption: Notes
notes.description: Additional notes related to the finding. Each note in the array can include a comment, the user who made the comment, the time when the note was created, and the time when the note was last modified (typically by the same user). Notes can be used to provide additional context, explanations, or observations by analysts related to the finding over time.

resources.caption: Affected Resources
resources.description: Describes details about resources that were the target of the activity that triggered the finding.

start_time.description: The time of the earliest event or finding that contributed to this finding.

time.description: The finding creation time — when the finding was first generated, not when the underlying activity occurred. For the time range of contributing events, use start_time and end_time.

type_uid_list.caption: Aggregate Types
type_uid_list.description: A unique list of type_uid values that represent the type of each group of findings when finding_info.related_events include other findings. This is a convenience that avoids walking the ist of finding_info.related_events to filter sets of similar findings that are not activities.

status.description: The finding lifecycle status label, normalized to the caption of the status_id value. When status_id is 99 (Other), this must contain the source-specific status label.

status_id.description: The normalized finding lifecycle status identifier. Unlike the status of an activity event, which indicates the success or failure of the activity, finding status tracks the review and triage workflow: whether the finding is new, being investigated, suppressed, or resolved. Producers should set this to reflect the current state of the finding in their system (e.g., 1 for newly created findings, 4 when remediated).

1.caption: New
1.description: The Finding is new and yet to be reviewed.

3.caption: Suppressed
3.description: The Finding was reviewed, determined to be benign or a false positive and is now suppressed.

4.caption: Resolved
4.description: The Finding was reviewed, remediated and is now considered resolved.

6.caption: Deleted
6.description: The Finding was deleted. For example, it might have been created in error.

users.description:
Describes the users that are affected by the finding.

If a single user is the source of the finding or activities behind the finding, use the Host profile and populate actor.user.
